description: §52 server-`!` error composes with §19.6 errorBoundary. loadName(id)! LoadError touches a server resource (escalates); the server response is the impl-neutral error directive {"__serverError": {type:"LoadError", variant:"Timeout"}} (the adapter translates to impl#1's wire envelope + HTTP 500 — see the §19.9.1-vs-impl#1 wire divergence flagged in the W3 report). Timeout has no `renders` clause, so the boundary `fallback=` markup shows (.eb-fallback). Siblings outside the boundary survive (§19.6.4: .eb-header + .eb-footer). The whole E-ERROR-* family stays silent — the boundary satisfies the handling requirement (§19.6.6). The server-fn twin of error-boundary/fallback (which uses a client failable fn).
rationale: §19.9.1 a server `!` function serializes its error variant across the boundary; §19.6.3 item 2 / §19.6.5 priority #2: a failing variant WITHOUT a `renders` clause falls through to the boundary `fallback`. Timeout (no renders) -> eb-fallback. §19.6.6: a `!`-call inside a boundary does NOT fire E-ERROR-* (the boundary handles it).
